name: The Guild Beggar-Spy
concept: No one sees a beggar. That is your whole power. You are a sworn member of the Beggars' Guild — the city's true eyes and ears — sitting invisible on every corner, running a hundred ragged children and cripples who between them see everything that happens in Lankhmar. You trade in the one thing worth more than stolen gold here: knowing.
duty: The Guild made you invisible, and invisibility is a leash. Every secret you gather, the Beggar King is owed a share of. Sell one behind the Guild's back at your peril.

look.label: Guise
options: a blind man's rags and cup
a filthy, forgettable street-child grown tall

look.label: Tell
options: a memory that forgets nothing said near you

gear.label: Network
options: a hidden blade among the rags (d6)

gear.label: Trove
options: a favor owed by someone very important

moves.name: Eyes on Every Corner
moves.trigger: When you put your beggars' network to work gathering word on a person, place, or plan.

results.success: Word comes back: you learn something true and useful about your subject.
results.failure: The word is thin, late, or bought — and someone knows you were asking.
advantage: Hold 1 Whisper (spend: a beggar saw exactly the thing you need / an urchin is already in place to watch or follow / a coded warning reaches you the moment danger moves / a secret on someone present, held in reserve).
You learn who else has been paying for word on the same subject.
threat: One of your people is seen, hurt, or turned; the network frays.
Add 1 Heat: your asking reaches an ear that reaches back.
results.triumph: The whole city's gossip pours in; you know your subject's business better than they do.
results.despair: Your network has been fed a lie, or infiltrated, and you're acting on poison.

hold.name: Whispers
spend: A beggar saw exactly the thing you now need to know.
An urchin is already in place to watch, follow, or carry a message.
A coded warning reaches you the instant danger moves.
You hold a secret on someone present, kept for the right moment.

moves.name: The Beggar King's Due
moves.trigger: When you call on the Beggars' Guild for shelter, a swarm of witnesses, a hidden road, or the city's gossip (Invoke Patron, core.md):
moves.text: The Guild provides — no one commands so many invisible eyes — and adds 1 Debt. The Beggar King is owed a share of every secret you gather; hoard from him, or sell what he has not been paid for, and you will find how quickly the invisible can become the hunted.

moves.name: Unseen in Plain Sight
moves.trigger: When you hide as what no one looks at — a beggar, a servant, a nothing — in plain view.

results.success: You are simply not noticed; you can watch, wait, or slip past as furniture.
results.failure: Someone looks twice, and the wrong someone.
advantage: Hide another in the crowd with you.
You overhear something worth the wait.
threat: Your guise slips a little; a detail is remembered.
You're in place, but boxed in.
results.triumph: You could stand in the room while they discuss killing you, and they'd never know.
results.despair: The one who sees through you is exactly the one who must not.

moves.name: A Hundred Faces
moves.trigger: When you take on a disguise or a false life to get somewhere you don't belong.

results.success: They take you for who you pretend to be, for now.
results.failure: The disguise won't hold here, or the wrong person knows the real you.
advantage: They buy it until you give them a hard reason not to.
You learn what only the disguised role would be told.
threat: The act costs you a prop, a coin, or a slip.
Someone files your false face away for later.
results.triumph: You become someone so convincingly you're welcomed and trusted.
results.despair: You've assumed the identity of someone with dangerous enemies, and just met one.

moves.name: Read the Street
moves.trigger: When you take the temper of a crowd, a quarter, or a room from its small signs.

results.success: Ask the GM one: who's afraid here? who's lying? where's the trouble about to start? Acting on it grants a boost die.
results.failure: You misread the mood, and it turns under you.
advantage: You spot the informant, the tail, or the trap in the crowd.
threat: Reading them, you were read in turn.
results.triumph: You feel the whole street's mood like a hand on a pulse.
results.despair: The calm you read was the calm before it fell on you.

moves.name: Trade in Secrets
moves.trigger: When you barter a secret you hold for coin, a favor, or another secret:
moves.text: Take a boost die; what you know is worth more here than gold. But every secret sold is a secret loosed, and the Beggar King expects his cut of the deal. The GM says who else learns you were dealing.

bonds: ____ is the only one in the band who ever really looks at me, rags and all.
____ thinks begging is beneath us and doesn't see the power in it; I let them underestimate it.
I have a secret on ____ I've never sold, and I'm not sure why I keep it.
____ runs errands for my network without knowing what they truly carry.
One of my urchins is ____'s kin, and that ties us tighter than either of us admits.
communityTie: The Black Cat always knows what's coming because you hear it first; ____ relies on your warnings.
The Beggar King would pay dearly for word from inside the band; ____ is the secret you will never sell him.
Little Wren the lookout is one of yours; you and ____ both quietly make sure the child stays safe.

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 the Beggar King makes a major demand, then resets to 3.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Whispers by 1.
Extend the network: name a quarter or a great house where your eyes now reach.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Buy down your Guild Debt by 1, permanently, with a secret the Beggar King prizes.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Go your own way: cut free of the Beggars' Guild and run your own web of eyes, resolving the fallout in play.
Rise toward the throne: build your own faction and a Bastion (a beggars' court) within the Guild.
